input.prompt: Your team is upgrading the codebase from .NET 9 to .NET 10. You have been asked to benchmark AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount to check how the upgrade will impact their performance. The source code is in the TextAnalyzer subdirectory (TextAnalyzer.cs and TextAnalyzer.csproj). We don't need anything too comprehensive, don't spend more than 10 minutes on this. Don't worry about cleaning up any files or projects you create along the way. Note: this machine may be under load from other processes. If the .NET 9 runtime is missing, install it using the official dotnet-install script from https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh (or dotnet-install.ps1 on Windows).

data.content: BDN 0.14.0 doesn't know .NET 10 yet. I'll run with `[SimpleJob]` on each framework separately.
data.reasoningText:
BenchmarkDotNet 0.14.0 may not have `RuntimeMoniker.Net100` for .NET 10. Let me check what's available. I could try `Net10_0` or look at the actual enum values. Actually, in newer BDN versions they use `Net10_0`. But 0.14.0 may not support .NET 10 at all. Let me check if there's a newer version available, or use a different approach.

Actually, a simpler approach: I can just use the `--runtimes` CLI argument for BenchmarkDotNet, or I can use a custom config. Or even simpler, I can just run the benchmarks twice - once on each runtime - using `--framework` flag.
 
I think the most straightforward solution is to just use a basic `[SimpleJob]` without specifying a runtime, then run the benchmark twice with different `--framework` flags. That way BDN runs on whatever runtime it's launched on. I'll skip the complexity of trying to get BDN to support .NET 10 directly and just run it twice to collect results from both runtimes.
